is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study analyzes pfcrt gene of Plasmodium falciparum isolates from Madagascar and the Comoro Islands.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports pfcrt K76T mutation (codon 76), a curated Pfcrt target position.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 gives counts: 0/40, 0/24, 0/19 in Madagascar; 33 (67%) of 49 in Comoro Islands; text notes 62% (30/48) of Comoro isolates harbored K76T.

sub_country_location.evidence: Sub-national resolution given: Mahajanga region of Madagascar (northwestern coast), Grande Comore/Moroni region of Comoro Islands, and isolate groups distributed across Madagascar island.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Collection dated 2000–2001 for Madagascar and 2001 for Comoro Islands (study performed February 2001), within 3 years.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field isolates from Madagascar and the Comoro Islands, both African/Indian Ocean countries; collected via national RER surveillance from malaria cases.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study comparing nucleotide sequences of 49 Comoro and 83 Madagascar isolates, published as a correspondence letter reporting original data.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples collected through national network 'Réseau d'Etude de la Résistance du Paludisme (RER)' surveillance and Madagascar national survey, raising possibility of overlap with other RER-derived publications.

supplement.evidence: K76T frequency counts and sub-national locations available in main text and Tables 1-2; no supplement pointer.

assessment.summary: Correspondence reporting pfcrt K76T frequencies in P. falciparum isolates from Madagascar (0/83 across island, Mahajanga region) and the Comoro Islands (33/49 = 67% in Grande Comore/Moroni region), collected 2000-2001 via the RER surveillance network. Reports extractable per-locus counts at sub-national resolution within a <=3-year window.
